ops.title: Strip the Claude Code baseline from the remaining LLM paths (profile distill, enrich extract)
ops.description: Spun off from lane B's refuter (task aade0a1c, shipped b34059d9): two production LLM call paths still carry the ~32K-token Claude Code baseline that cached_judge now strips — (1) cc_steer/profile.py's structured_judge(DistilledProfile, ...) distill call (route it through cached_judge, same pattern); (2) enrich's cc_transcript.extract.extract_correction path (cc_steer/enrich.py:142) — this fix lives in cc-transcript (cross-repo, per the push-reusable-machinery convention). Enrich is ENRICH_LIMIT=200 rows/night at sonnet, so the win is real but smaller than the judge lanes'. Gate any behavior-affecting change the same way lane B did: cost + equivalence vs a self-agreement floor. CAUTION from lane B's evidence: do not set max_turns on any --json-schema path (structured output is a tool_use round, num_turns 2-4).

ops.text: profile distill routes through cached_judge with measured token drop
cc-transcript extract path strips the baseline (cross-repo change) or a recorded decision not to
equivalence gate passes for any path whose outputs feed persisted records
